claudeResult.resultText:
The signup form is on the canvas at slot `slot_mri7opwa_pvv0o6`.

Structure: a Card wrapping a vertical Stack with three Inputs and a right-aligned "Sign up" Button. Each input is controlled via `$bindState` against the seeded `state.form` object (`name`, `email`, `password` all seeded to `""`), so the fields render controlled from the first paint and edits flow back into state. The email input uses `type: "email"`, the password input uses `type: "password"` with `minLength: 8` plus a description line, and all three are marked `required`. The button fires `canvas.submit` with the whole `form` object as the payload.

One thing worth flagging: the catalog has no `Form` component (my first attempt used one and was rejected), so there's no native form element handling submit-time validation. The `required` and `minLength` props are per-input; whether they block submission depends on how the runtime enforces them, so if you want a hard guarantee, I can disable the button until the state satisfies the rules.

I couldn't take a snapshot to verify the rendered layout — no canvas tab is connected. Open http://localhost:7814/?session=d9f22892-0917-42a0-9c79-659ae1075145 and I'll capture one.
